name: Tierra de Campos Castles Route
name_original: Melgar de Abajo desde Ampudia – circular por Mayorga y Medina de Rioseco
description: This difficult 82.1-mile road cycling route from Ampudia explores the Tierra de Campos region, passing through Mayorga and Medina de Rioseco
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Ampudia traverse the flat to gently rolling plains of Tierra de Campos, characterized by open agricultural landscapes and historical castles. The region features minimal elevation changes, with most routes offering gradual ascents and descents. Cyclists can expect to encounter a mix of cultivated fields and occasional wetlands, such as the Laguna de la Nava. This terrain provides opportunities for long-distance rides with consistent pacing. Best road cycling routes around Ampudia The most popular road cycling route is Tierra de Campos and Montealegre Castle Loop, a 48.1 miles (77.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 8 minutes to complete. This route explores the open plains and includes a historical castle. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Villamartin de Campos from Ampudia – circular by the Laguna de la Nava, a moderate 44.4 miles (71.4 km) path. This circular route offers views of the Laguna de la Nava wetland area. Local road cyclists also love the Tierra de Campos Castles Route, an 82.1 miles (132.2 km) trail leading through the historical castles of the Tierra de Campos region, often completed in about 5 hours 20 minutes. Road cycling around Ampudia is defined by open plains, historical castles, and wetland areas. The network offers options for moderate and difficult routes, catering to various experience levels. The routes in Ampudia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 70 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Ampudia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the overall difficulty level of road cycling routes around Ampudia?
A: The road cycling routes in Ampudia primarily feature moderate difficulty, with five out of six routes falling into this category. There is also one more challenging route available for experienced cyclists. The terrain is generally flat to gently rolling, characterized by minimal elevation changes across open agricultural landscapes.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes in the Ampudia area?
A: Yes, Ampudia offers several options for longer rides. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1166614995/tierra-de-campos-castles-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tierra de Campos Castles Route&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 82.1-mile (132.2 km) trail, typically completed in about 5 hours 20 minutes. Another substantial option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20149287" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ampudia Castle – Castle of Ampudia loop from Cabezón de Pisuerga&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 82.2 km.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect while road cycling around Ampudia?
A: Road cycling around Ampudia offers expansive views of the Tierra de Campos region, known for its open agricultural plains and historical castles. You'll encounter cultivated fields, and some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1166554837/villamartin-de-campos-from-ampudia-circular-by-the-laguna-de-la-nava" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villamartin de Campos from Ampudia – circular by the Laguna de la Nava&lt;/a&gt;, provide views of wetland areas such as the Laguna de la Nava. For panoramic vistas, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3464062" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Fields of Tierra de Campos&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3178603" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tierra de Campos Viewpoint (Autilla del Pino)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available in Ampudia?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are designed as loops. A popular circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1166554837/villamartin-de-campos-from-ampudia-circular-by-the-laguna-de-la-nava" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villamartin de Campos from Ampudia – circular by the Laguna de la Nava&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 44.4-mile (71.4 km) path. Another loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1166490018/tierra-de-campos-and-montealegre-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tierra de Campos and Montealegre Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt;, covering 48.1 miles (77.4 km).

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in history, featuring numerous castles. You can explore routes that pass by or lead to sites such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1789312" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montealegre de Campos Castle&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3744020" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Trigueros del Valle&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5816412" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Ampudia&lt;/a&gt; itself. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1166614995/tierra-de-campos-castles-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tierra de Campos Castles Route&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to highlight these historical structures.

Q: What is the best season for road cycling in Ampudia?
A: Given the open agricultural landscapes, spring and autumn are generally ideal for road cycling in Ampudia. These seasons offer milder temperatures and pleasant scenery, with autumn bringing unique colors to the fields. Summer can be quite hot, while winter might be chilly and potentially wet.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly road cycling routes around Ampudia?
A: While there are no routes specifically graded as 'easy' in the current selection, the region's generally flat to gently rolling terrain makes many moderate routes accessible for beginners looking for a longer ride. The minimal elevation changes allow for consistent pacing, which can be suitable for those building endurance.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Ampudia?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the open plains, the historical castles, and the unique wetland areas like Laguna de la Nava, which provide a distinct cycling experience.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through towns or villages where I could find coffee stops?
A: Many routes in the Ampudia region connect various villages and towns, offering opportunities for refreshment. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20149330" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santiago de Cigales – Bodegas District of Mucientes loop from Cigales&lt;/a&gt; passes through the Bodegas District of Mucientes, where you might find local establishments. Planning your stops in advance, especially in smaller villages, is always recommended.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Ampudia area?
A: There are currently six distinct road cycling routes available around Ampudia, offering a variety of distances and experiences across the Tierra de Campos region.

Q: Can I find routes that explore specific cultural or religious sites?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate significant cultural landmarks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20149330" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santiago de Cigales – Bodegas District of Mucientes loop from Cigales&lt;/a&gt; specifically features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5818970" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santiago de Cigales&lt;/a&gt;, allowing cyclists to combine their ride with a visit to this religious building.
tips: Santervás de Campos is a small town in the Tierra de Campos region next to the Valderaduey river. The town is famous for being located at a crossroads on the Camino de Santiago between the Camino Frances and the Camino de Madrid.

A small rural town in Valladolid that borders the province of León on the Cea River. The most notable buildings in the town are its two churches. The church of San Juan from the 18th century and the church of El Salvador from the 16th century.

Mayorga is an important regional town located on the banks of the Cea River in the province of Valladolid. The most notable monuments of the town from Roman times are the red brick buildings, especially near the Plaza de España such as the Town Hall and the Casa de Cachón, former Palace of the Pimentels.

Medina de Rioseco is known as the seat of the Admiralty of Castilla, the city has a rich history that goes back to the Vacceos, Celts, Romans. Located next to the sequillo river, I recommend you tour the town by bike and visit the Church of Santiago Apóstol.
You can see the hilltop castle from miles away dominating the landscape. The Castle of Montealegre de Campos dates from the 10th century.
One of the jewels of the Tierra de Campos regions in Palencia, the historic cultural city of Ampudia is a must-see. With an impressive castle and heritage townscape.

faqs.question: How much time should I allocate for this circular road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This is a challenging route designed for racebikes, covering approximately 132 kilometers. While the estimated moving time is around 5 hours and 20 minutes, you should plan for a full day, especially if you intend to stop at any of the historical sites or for refreshments. Consider your fitness level and desired pace when planning your total duration.

faqs.question: Where is the best place to park when starting this route in Ampudia?
faqs.answer: Ampudia is a convenient starting point for this circular route. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, you can typically find parking within the town center or near local amenities. Look for designated public parking areas or inquire locally upon arrival. The area is generally less congested, offering easier parking than larger cities.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for road cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The route traverses the varied geography of Castilla y León, specifically the Tierra de Campos area. You'll encounter extensive plains and gentle hills, with long stretches through rural landscapes and vast agricultural fields. Roads are generally well-maintained and often less traveled, making for a pleasant road cycling experience. Expect a mix of flat sections and moderate climbs, contributing to its 'difficult' rating.

faqs.question: What historical and cultural sights can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: This route offers a rich historical and cultural immersion. You'll pass through charming towns and villages with significant landmarks. Highlights include the Castle of Ampudia, the Church of San Juan of Melgar de Abajo, the Mayorga Town Center, and the Church of Santiago Apóstol, Medina de Rioseco. Near Medina de Rioseco, you can also glimpse the historic Canal de Castilla. Further along, you might spot the impressive Montealegre Castle.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners or families with young children?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating, a distance of over 130 kilometers, and moderate elevation gain, this route is best suited for experienced road cyclists with good fitness levels. It is not recommended for beginners or families with young children. Shorter, flatter routes can be found in the Castilla y León region for those seeking an easier experience.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle in Castilla y León, particularly in the Tierra de Campos area, is during the spring (April to June) and early autumn (September to October). During these months, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, with less extreme heat than summer and fewer cold temperatures or heavy rains than winter. This allows for comfortable long-distance cycling.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this public road route through the Tierra de Campos region. You are free to enjoy the roads and scenery. However, individual attractions like castles or museums along the way may have their own entrance fees if you choose to visit them.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with several other paths. You will find sections that overlap with the Madrid Way of Saint James, the Tierra de Campos trail, and the Cañada Real Leonesa Occidental. Near Medina de Rioseco, the route also touches upon the Canal de Castilla (Ramal de Medina) and the GR-89 BTT Canal de Castilla (Ramal de Medina).

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While there are no specific restrictions against having dogs accompany you on public roads, this route is designed for racebikes and covers a significant distance. It's generally not ideal for dogs to run alongside a cyclist for such a long duration. If you plan to stop in towns, be aware of local regulations regarding pets in establishments or public spaces.

faqs.question: Are there places to find food, drinks, or accommodation along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through several towns like Ampudia, Mayorga, and Medina de Rioseco, you will find opportunities for refreshments and accommodation. These towns offer cafes, restaurants, and various lodging options. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially between towns, as some rural stretches may have limited services.

faqs.question: What is the main advantage of choosing this specific circular route?
faqs.answer: The main advantage of this circular route is the unique blend of tranquility and cultural immersion it offers. You'll experience authentic rural Spain on less-traveled, well-maintained roads, away from heavy traffic. The flexibility of a circular route allows you to start and end in the same location, while the diverse landscapes and numerous historical sites provide a visually engaging and enriching cycling journey through the heart of Castilla y León.
